conflicts.description: Chapter 1 presents the wedding reception as the Marriott ballroom in Worthington with about 180 guests, but chapter 2 later compresses it into the Columbus Athenaeum with eighty guests.
conflicts.canonical_value: Kelsey and Tyler's wedding reception takes place at the Marriott ballroom in Worthington with about 180 guests.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 1 is the direct video-viewing scene and anchors the event details; the Athenaeum/eighty-guest phrasing appears once later in summary language and is easier to revise.

conflicts.description: Doug's recurring Westerville pool-and-roses claim changes across retellings: the underlying file says February and about 6,000 gallons, while later versions shift the month, water volume, and coverage path.
conflicts.canonical_value: Doug's canonical example claim is the detailed chapter 3 version: a February failure of a Westerville above-ground pool that sends about 6,000 gallons into Margaret Commonwealth's rose garden, destroying 17 hybrid tea roses valued at $4,218.47.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 3 presents the actual file in full claims detail; later versions are compressed performance retellings and are less causally embedded.

conflicts.description: Layla's venue fact sheet says the Lincoln Theatre seats 582, while Doug notes the Netflix contract and repeated house counts use 580.
conflicts.canonical_value: The Lincoln Theatre continuity capacity is 580 seats.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript explicitly labels 582 as Layla's wrong number and repeats 580 in rehearsal and taping scenes.

conflicts.description: Doug says the second bedroom contains six boxes during the taping, but chapter 18's literal room inventory counts seven and names the labels.
conflicts.canonical_value: The second bedroom contains seven labeled boxes, including one marked KELSEY.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 18 is the physical post-premiere inventory of the room and is more deeply embedded than the compressed onstage line.

conflicts.description: The previous sheet only estimated Doug's Mutual Fidelity salary, but the manuscript repeats an exact pre-tax figure.
conflicts.canonical_value: Doug earns $61,400 per year before taxes at Mutual Fidelity.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript states the exact number multiple times, so the approximate prior-sheet value is stale and should be tightened.

conflicts.description: The previous sheet stretched the novel to roughly 5-6 months, but the manuscript moves from a Sunday in February to a Monday in June.
conflicts.canonical_value: The story spans about four months, from February through June.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript's seasonal markers stay consistent across the book and compress the action to roughly four months.
